mislead_scenario: The user asks for a pre-mortem on a project plan, and the AI agent attempts to facilitate a synchronous, multi-person physical meeting (e.g., 'Gather the team', 'Silent brainstorming for 5-7 min', 'Go around the room') instead of adapting the exercise for an asynchronous or two-party human-AI interaction.
missing_boundaries: When the agent is working autonomously on a task and cannot facilitate a group meeting
When interacting with a single developer (the process must be adapted, not run as a group exercise)
weakest_part: The Process section, which rigidly assumes a synchronous, multi-human meeting format that an AI CLI agent cannot actually execute.
highest_leverage_fix: Rewrite the 'Process' section into a turn-based Human-AI protocol where the AI agent acts as a contrarian, generating adversarial failure reasons based on its codebase context, and prompts the solo user to contribute theirs.
